name: pre-commit-fixer
purpose: Diagnose and fix pre-commit hook failures through a structured classify-fix-report-confirm workflow with user gates at every step.

existing_metadata.short-description: Diagnose + fix pre-commit hook failures with confirmation
existing_metadata.lineage: Upstream pre-commit-fixer v2.0 → JASON-OS v0.1 port

notes: Foundation-scoped to gitleaks-only hook. Additional recipe categories (ESLint, Prettier, tsc, lint-staged, pattern-compliance, etc.) are armed but inactive until Post-Foundation Deferrals land. Version numbering mixes upstream (2.0) and JASON-OS port (v0.1) — may confuse SoNash cross-reference. No REFERENCE.md — single-file skill. References /systematic-debugging and /quick-fix as deferred upstream neighbors not yet ported.

name: session-begin
purpose: Pre-flight checklist that loads context, validates environment, surfaces warnings, and hands off to the user with a goal-selection prompt at the start of every work session.
notes: Heavily DEFERRED in JASON-OS v0.1 — Phases 3-4 (health scripts, warning ack gate, infrastructure failure gate, debt snapshot) all marked DEFERRED pending Layer 2 hooks. REFERENCE.md exists but is short (routing table + anti-patterns pointer). Scope_hint is 'project' rather than 'universal' because the SESSION_CONTEXT.md 5-field contract and BOOTSTRAP_DEFERRED.md pointers are JASON-OS-specific conventions; the session-management concept is universal but the artifact contracts are project-shaped. Portability requires rewiring DEFERRED markers to destination project's infrastructure.

name: session-end
purpose: Session closure pipeline that preserves context in SESSION_CONTEXT.md, runs optional compliance review, and commits all changes so the next session starts with accurate state.

existing_metadata.lineage: SoNash 41526 session-end v2.2 → JASON-OS v0.1 port

notes: Phase 3 (metrics pipeline) stripped entirely for v0. Phase 2 (compliance review) mostly Layer-2-gated — silently skips in v0. Phase 1 + 4 fully wired. Hardcoded plan-file target .planning/jason-os-mvp/PLAN.md (D33) — will need updating when a second planning directory exists. References scripts/session-end-commit.js which must exist in destination. Learning loop writes to memory/session-end-learnings.md. Scope_hint 'project' for same reason as session-begin — SESSION_CONTEXT.md 5-field contract is JASON-OS/SoNash convention.

name: skill-audit
purpose: Interactive behavioral quality audit for individual skills, evaluating against 12 quality categories to surface attention-management issues and produce a decision record with implemented fixes.
notes: Largest skill by disk size (65 KB). SKILL.md is 599 lines — significantly over the 300-line standard the skill itself enforces; relies on companion REFERENCE.md (which is itself 928 lines) to carry the 12-category rubrics. Three modes: single (gated), batch (one skill batched), multi (many skills). npm run skills:validate not wired in JASON-OS v0 — substituted with manual structural check. Version history shows this is still the upstream/SoNash version (no JASON-OS port suffix in version field, unlike pre-commit-fixer and session-end). Portable as-is to SoNash — no project-specific refs. Needs scripts/skills/skill-audit/self-audit.js to exist.

name: skill-creator
purpose: Structured workflow for creating or updating skills, from discovery through validation and audit, producing structurally correct and behaviorally effective skill packages.
notes: References init_skill.py which does not appear to exist in JASON-OS (Phase 4.2 scaffold). References npm run skills:validate which is not wired in JASON-OS v0 (noted in skill-audit but not flagged here). References ROADMAP.md — JASON-OS v0 does not have a ROADMAP.md yet. References .research/EXTRACTIONS.md and extraction-journal.jsonl — likely absent in fresh installs. References docs/agent_docs/SKILL_AGENT_POLICY.md — needs verification. Also references /create-audit skill which does not appear in JASON-OS skill inventory. REFERENCE.md carries 12-item anti-pattern list and full content checklist. Portability flag: sanitize-then-portable because several referenced scripts/files (init_skill.py, ROADMAP.md, EXTRACTIONS.md) do not exist in JASON-OS v0.

name: todo
purpose: Cross-session todo management with an 8-option interactive menu, JSONL-backed persistence via a locked CLI, and AI-powered context capture so ideas survive across sessions.
notes: Portability is 'sanitize-then-portable' because the skill is deeply dependent on scripts/planning/todos-cli.js (locked, regression-guarded) and scripts/planning/render-todos.js — these must exist and be functional. The T30 fix (routing all mutations through the CLI) is critical to correctness; bypassing it causes data loss. References /gsd:add-todo as a neighbor — GSD is a separate skill system that may not exist in destination. References /task-next which is not in JASON-OS skill inventory (deferred or SoNash-only). The .planning/todos.jsonl path is hardcoded — projects with different planning layouts would need adjustment.
